_comment: Cognitive OS hooks portability shim for Cursor IDE (hooks v1, Cursor 1.7+).
_note: Hook scripts are identical to Claude Code. Only this registration config differs.
_variable: Cursor does not expose a $PROJECT_DIR variable — hooks run with cwd = workspace root, so relative paths work. Use shell expansion for portability.

_gaps.SessionStart: Cursor has no sessionStart lifecycle event. Use 'beforeSubmitPrompt' as the nearest substitute for first-prompt setup, but it fires on every prompt — guard with a flag file if needed.
_gaps.PreCompact: Cursor has no context-compaction lifecycle event. pre-compaction-flush.sh cannot be wired here.
_gaps.PostToolUse_Agent: Cursor has no sub-agent concept. Hooks that fire on Claude Code 'Agent' tool use are mapped to 'afterFileEdit' or 'stop' as best-effort approximations.
_gaps.matcher_Bash_only: Cursor's 'beforeShellExecution' covers all shell use. Bash-specific matchers from Claude Code map 1:1.
_gaps.matcher_Agent_only: No direct equivalent. Agent-scoped hooks from Claude Code are mapped to 'stop' (post-turn) as the closest Cursor lifecycle point.

beforeSubmitPrompt._comment: Nearest substitute for SessionStart — fires on every prompt, not only session open. Guards should be used in the scripts themselves (flag files / lock files).

beforeShellExecution._comment: Maps to PreToolUse[Bash] in Claude Code — rate limiting and loop detection for shell commands.
Resource / dispatch gates — originally Agent-scoped in Claude Code but applied broadly here since Cursor has no agent sub-process concept.

beforeReadFile._comment: Maps to PreToolUse[Read] — no direct Claude Code equivalent registered, but large-file advisor is useful here.

afterFileEdit._comment: Maps to PostToolUse[Edit|Write] — security and quality gates that run after every file write.
Crash-recovery checkpoint — maps to PostToolUse[Bash|Edit|Write] in Claude Code.

beforeMCPExecution._comment: Maps to PreToolUse[Agent] in Claude Code — validation gates before any MCP/agent tool use.

stop._comment: Maps to Stop hooks AND PostToolUse[Agent] hooks — fires at end of each agent turn.
stop._comment_error_pipeline: Bash PostToolUse error pipeline — approximated at turn end (no per-command granularity in Cursor).

stop._comment: Agent PostToolUse hooks — quality and consequence evaluation at turn end.
